title: Make pcb_component anchors optional and add "relative_to_another_component" position_mode
description: Adds optional anchor metadata for flexible component placement and a new position mode for referencing other components in PCB design.
body:
### Motivation
- Allow components to specify optional anchor metadata for more flexible placement and reference other components when positioning by adding `anchor_position` and `anchor_alignment` and a new `position_mode` option.

### Description
- Added `anchor_position: point.optional()` and `anchor_alignment: ninePointAnchor.optional()` to the Zod schema in `src/pcb/pcb_component.ts` and imported `ninePointAnchor` from `src/common/NinePointAnchor`.
- Added `"relative_to_another_component"` to the `position_mode` enum in the Zod schema and to the exported `PcbComponent` TypeScript interface.
- Updated documentation in `README.md` and `docs/PCB_COMPONENT_OVERVIEW.md` to reflect the new optional fields and `position_mode` value.
- Extended `tests/pcb_component_position_mode.test.ts` to include the new `position_mode` value and a test that validates the optional anchor fields parse correctly.

### Testing
- Ran `bun test tests/pcb_component_position_mode.test.ts` which passed (11 tests, 0 failures).
- Ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` to typecheck the project which succeeded with no errors.
- Ran `bun run format` which formatted the repository files successfully.

title: Add optional DRC-disable flags to platformConfig
description: Add optional boolean flags to the PlatformConfig interface to disable specific DRC checks (netlist, routing, placement) for downstream tooling.
body:
### Motivation
- Provide platform-level toggles to disable specific DRC checks (netlist, routing, placement) so downstream tooling can opt out of those validations via configuration.
- Keep runtime validation and published docs in sync by adding the fields to the TypeScript interface and the Zod schema so config parsing preserves these options.

### Description
- Added `netlistDrcChecksDisabled`, `routingDrcChecksDisabled`, and `placementDrcChecksDisabled` as optional boolean properties to the `PlatformConfig` TypeScript interface in `lib/platformConfig.ts`.
- Updated the `platformConfig` Zod schema in `lib/platformConfig.ts` to accept and validate the three optional boolean flags.
- Added a unit test `tests/platformConfig.drcChecksDisabled.test.ts` that verifies the new fields are accepted and preserved by `platformConfig.parse`.
- Regenerated docs so `README.md` and `generated/PROPS_OVERVIEW.md` include the new fields and ran formatting and typechecks.

### Testing

title: Fix duplicate build logs shown during live streaming
description: Fixes the issue of duplicate build logs being displayed during live streaming by ensuring that completed logs are only shown after streaming finishes.
body:

- When a build is active the UI rendered both persisted `user_code_job_completed_logs` and incoming SSE `streamedLogs`, causing duplicate log lines (one with formatted timestamps and one without) during live streaming while reloads only showed the completed, formatted logs.

### Description
- Gate rendering of `packageBuild.user_code_job_completed_logs` behind `!userCodeJobInProgress` in both `ReleaseBuildLogs` and `SingleBuildLogs` so completed logs are only shown after streaming finishes.

title: feat: add pseudo-orthographic camera preset and normalize direction function
description: Adds a new camera preset for a pseudo-orthographic top-down view and a normalizeDir function to ensure direction vectors are consistently normalized for improved camera positioning.
body:
This pull request enhances the camera preset utilities by improving direction normalization and adding a new camera preset for a pseudo-orthographic top-down view. The most significant changes are grouped below:

**Camera direction handling:**

* Added a `normalizeDir` helper function to ensure all direction vectors are normalized, improving numerical stability and consistency in camera positioning.
* Updated `repositionCamera` to use the new normalization function and support an optional distance override, allowing more flexible camera repositioning.

**Camera presets:**

* Added a new `"top-down-ortho"` camera preset that simulates an orthographic projection by reducing the field of view and adjusting the camera distance accordingly.
* Slightly adjusted the direction vector in the `"top-down"` preset for improved accuracy.

title: feat: add camera presets for 3D snapshots and validation
description: Adds support for camera angle presets when generating 3D snapshots via the CLI, allowing users to select different viewpoints for rendered images.
body:
This pull request adds support for camera angle presets when generating 3D snapshots via the CLI, allowing users to select different viewpoints for rendered images. It introduces a new shared module for camera presets, updates the snapshot logic to apply these presets, and includes comprehensive tests to ensure correct behavior and error handling.

https://linear.app/tscircuit/issue/TSC-397/camera-presets-for-tsci-snapshot-3d-snapshots

**Camera Preset Feature:**

* Added a new `lib/shared/camera-presets.ts` module that defines multiple named camera angle presets, a type-safe API for applying them, and validation utilities. This enables consistent, reusable camera positioning for 3D renders.
* The CLI `snapshot` command now accepts a `--camera-preset <preset>` option, validates the preset, and applies it to 3D renders. Using this option automatically enables 3D snapshot generation. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-56575631625ab591c4c0e55f18db3fd06c8ec250d28200a8dcab0a035a573a73R2-R5) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-56575631625ab591c4c0e55f18db3fd06c8ec250d28200a8dcab0a035a573a73R24-R27) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-56575631625ab591c4c0e55f18db3fd06c8ec250d28200a8dcab0a035a573a73R40-R54) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-56575631625ab591c4c0e55f18db3fd06c8ec250d28200a8dcab0a035a573a73R65)

**Snapshot Logic Updates:**

* The `snapshotProject` function in `lib/shared/snapshot-project.ts` is updated to accept an optional `cameraPreset` parameter, which, if provided, overrides the default camera angle for 3D renders and ensures 3D output is generated. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-2092324af8269cfb0e148140806e9cd2bdaa13d8e031df470ee0204bc17b26b9R9) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-2092324af8269cfb0e148140806e9cd2bdaa13d8e031df470ee0204bc17b26b9R45-R46) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-2092324af8269cfb0e148140806e9cd2bdaa13d8e031df470ee0204bc17b26b9R65-R70) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-2092324af8269cfb0e148140806e9cd2bdaa13d8e031df470ee0204bc17b26b9L165-R176)

**Testing:**

* Added a new test suite (`tests/cli/snapshot/snapshot-camera-preset.test.ts`) that verifies all camera presets generate valid 3D snapshots, checks for correct error handling with invalid presets, ensures `--camera-preset` implies 3D output, and confirms that different presets produce distinct images.

title: Optimize SingleHighDensityRouteSolver obstacle checks with Flatbush
description: Optimizes obstacle checks in the SingleHighDensityRouteSolver by implementing a spatial index using Flatbush, reducing computational overhead during routing iterations.
body:
### Motivation
- The A* node-expansion hotspot was performing full scans of every obstacle route on every neighbor expansion, causing per-iteration cost to grow with obstacle count.
- Introduce a spatial index to reduce per-step work by restricting geometric tests to nearby obstacle primitives.

### Description
- Add Flatbush-backed spatial indexes for obstacle segments and vias and precompute per-segment connectivity flags via a new `buildObstacleIndexes()` call in the constructor.
- Replace full-array scans in `isNodeTooCloseToObstacle` with a bbox query against `obstacleSegmentIndex` / `obstacleViaIndex` followed by exact `pointToSegmentDistance` / `distance` checks for returned candidates, preserving layer and connected-net semantics.
- Replace full-array scans in `doesPathToParentIntersectObstacle` with a bbox query and then call `doSegmentsIntersect` only on nearby segments.
- Add focused unit tests `tests/single-high-density-route-solver.test.ts` that validate indexed detection of segment/via proximity and that connected obstacles are ignored for clearance and intersection checks.

### Testing
- Ran `bun test tests/single-high-density-route-solver.test.ts` and it passed (2 tests, 0 failures).
- Ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` for type checking and it completed successfully.
- Ran `bun run format` which formatted files successfully.

title: Add workflow_dispatch for benchmark and exclude assignable solvers by default
description: Add workflow_dispatch to benchmark workflow allowing manual benchmark runs with options for solver name, scenario limit, concurrency, and inclusion of assignable pipelines, while excluding assignable solvers by default.
body:
### Motivation

- Allow running the benchmark manually from the GitHub UI and provide an option to opt into assignable pipelines while keeping them excluded by default to speed typical runs.
- Make it possible to pass solver, scenario limit, concurrency, ref, and assignable inclusion from both issue comments and manual dispatch so CI runs are consistent.

### Description

- Add `workflow_dispatch` to `.github/workflows/benchmark.yml` with inputs `solver_name`, `scenario_limit`, `concurrency`, `include_assignable`, and `ref`, and extend parsing to accept these inputs. 
- Wire a new `include_assignable` output from the workflow parser into the Run step so the job can pass `--include-assignable` to the shell runner when requested.
- Change `benchmark.sh` to exclude assignable pipelines by default using an `INCLUDE_ASSIGNABLE` toggle and add a `--include-assignable` CLI option to opt in. 
- Update `scripts/benchmark/index.ts` to accept a new `--exclude-assignable` flag and filter solver names (removing names containing `Assignable`) when exclusion is requested.

### Testing

- Ran TypeScript typecheck with `bunx tsc --noEmit` and it succeeded. 
- Ran formatter with `bun run format` which completed successfully. 
- Validated shell script syntax with `bash -n benchmark.sh` which passed. 
- Executed `./benchmark.sh --help` to confirm usage and solver listing behavior and it printed expected help and filtered solver list.

body:
### Motivation

- Provide a simple utility to compute a PCB board's bounds/size from either explicit `width`/`height`/`center` or from an `outline` polygon. 
- Make the package API discoverable by documenting every exported function near the top of the README.

### Description

- Added `getBoardBounds(board)` in `lib/get-board-bounds.ts` which returns `{ minX, minY, maxX, maxY, width, height, center }` and uses `width`/`height`/`center` when available or falls back to computing from `outline`; it throws a clear error if neither representation exists. 
- Exported the new function from the package entrypoint via `index.ts` so it is available to consumers. 
- Added tests in `tests/get-board-bounds.test.ts` covering both the `width/height/center` and `outline` computation paths. 
- Inserted an `## Exported API` table near the top of `README.md` that documents each exported function with a link to the defining file and a short description.

title: Support implicit single-pin component selectors for traces (localized fallback)
description: Adds a localized fallback for resolving single-pin component selectors in traces without altering global selector semantics.
body:
### Motivation
- Users should be able to refer to single-pin components by name (e.g. `TP1`) when connecting traces without changing global selector semantics.  
- Prior attempt modified `selectOne`/caching broadly and was fragile; a minimal, localized approach is safer.  

### Description
- Add a localized fallback in `Trace__findConnectedPorts` that, when a trace endpoint selector fails to resolve as a port and does not explicitly contain a port token, resolves the selector as a component and returns its sole `Port` if the component has exactly one port.  
- The fallback is implemented as `resolveImplicitSinglePort(selector)` and is only used when `selectOne(selector, { type: 'port' })` returns `null`.  
- Add tests to cover the intended behavior: `single-pin-component-implies-pin1.test.tsx` (accept single-pin implicit selector) and `multi-pin-component-no-implied-pin1-trace-error.test.tsx` (ensure multi-pin components do not imply `pin1`).  
- Add updated PCB/schematic snapshot artifacts for the single-pin test.  

### Testing
- Ran `BUN_UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 bun test tests/components/base-components/single-pin-component-implies-pin1.test.tsx` and the test passed with snapshots created.  
- Ran `bun test tests/components/base-components/multi-pin-component-no-implied-pin1-trace-error.test.tsx tests/components/base-components/trace-port-selector-errors.test.tsx tests/components/base-components/shorthand-selector.test.tsx` and all tests passed.  
- Ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` for type-checking and `bun run format`; both completed successfully.

body:
### Motivation

- Make PCB footprint overlap diagnostics more actionable by including clearer identifiers and spatial context for overlapping elements to simplify debugging of errors like `pcb_smtpad element overlaps with pcb_smtpad element`.
- Provide a small, reproducible test in the same JSX/style as the provided sample circuit to ensure the new message format is emitted and rendered.

### Description

- Added a `formatOverlapElementDescription` helper that produces a descriptive string for an overlapped element including readable name + element id, owning component (when present), port (when present), and center coordinates in millimeters, and wired it into the overlap error `message` creation in `checkPcbComponentOverlap`.
- Expanded imports to use `getReadableNameForComponent` and `getReadableNameForPort` and replaced the terse overlap message with the new detailed format.
- Added a repro test `tests/lib/check-pcb-component-overlap/test.tsx` that renders a small board/chip footprint (sample003-style) to reproduce a `pcb_smtpad` overlap and added/updated SVG snapshots to assert the error is drawn.

### Testing

- Ran `BUN_UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 bun test tests/lib/check-pcb-component-overlap` and the suite passed (4 tests, snapshots refreshed for overlap cases).
- Ran `BUN_UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 bun test ./tests/lib/check-pcb-component-overlap/test.tsx` and the new repro test passed and wrote the snapshot (1 pass).
- Ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` for type-checking and it completed successfully with no errors.
- Ran `bun run format`, which completed successfully but reported Biome size warnings for large fixture JSON files while formatting the repository.

title: feat(build): support --inject-props and --inject-props-file in tsci build
description: Enable callers of tsci build to inject a JSON props object into the default export of a component at build-time, a common need when adjusting behavior like autorouting without editing source files.
body:
### Motivation
- Enable callers of `tsci build` to inject a JSON props object into the default export of a component at build-time, a common need when adjusting behavior like autorouting without editing source files.

### Description
- Add CLI options `--inject-props <json>` and `--inject-props-file <path>` and parsing/validation logic that ensures the injected value is a JSON object and prevents using both flags together via `parseInjectedProps` in `cli/build/register.ts`.
- Thread the parsed `injectedProps` through build machinery (sequential and worker paths) by extending `BuildCommandOptions`, `BuildJob` options, worker message types, and the worker pool so both single-threaded and concurrent builds receive the props (`cli/build/build-ci.ts`, `cli/build/register.ts`, `cli/build/worker-types.ts`, `cli/build/worker-pool.ts`, `cli/build/build-worker-entrypoint.ts`).
- Apply injected props when rendering the component during circuit generation by passing `injectedProps` into `generateCircuitJson` and rendering as `<Component {...(injectedProps ?? {})} />` (`lib/shared/generate-circuit-json.tsx`), and ensure `buildFile` forwards the option (`cli/build/build-file.ts`).
- Add integration tests that exercise both inline JSON and file-based injection and assert the produced `circuit.json` reflects the injected props (`tests/cli/build/build-inject-props.test.ts`).

### Testing
- Ran `bun test tests/cli/build/build-inject-props.test.ts` and the two new tests passed (2 passed, 0 failed).
- Ran TypeScript checking with `bunx tsc --noEmit` which succeeded with no errors.
- Ran code formatting with `bun run format` which completed successfully.

title: feat: add support for JST ZH connector and update tests
description: Adds support for the JST ZH connector family (1.5mm pitch wire-to-board) to the footprint generator, including updates to type definitions, parsing logic, pad and silkscreen generation, and tests.
body:
This pull request adds support for the JST ZH connector family (1.5mm pitch wire-to-board) to the footprint generator. The changes include updates to the type definitions, parsing logic, pad and silkscreen generation, as well as tests and input normalization to handle ZH connectors.

**JST ZH connector support:**

* Added an optional `zh` boolean property to `jst_def`, updated the `JstVariant` type, and included ZH-specific default dimensions in `variantDefaults`. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-de396367ba8a34cbf02f41b8ff546dc8155eca135368a5beee7c2e204ad54055R35-R48) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-de396367ba8a34cbf02f41b8ff546dc8155eca135368a5beee7c2e204ad54055R66-R79)
* Updated pad generation logic in `generatePads` to handle the ZH variant with its specific dimensions.
* Modified silkscreen body generation in `generateSilkscreenBody` to draw the ZH connector outline based on pin count and pitch. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-de396367ba8a34cbf02f41b8ff546dc8155eca135368a5beee7c2e204ad54055R142-R143) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-de396367ba8a34cbf02f41b8ff546dc8155eca135368a5beee7c2e204ad54055R160-R180)
* Enhanced string parsing in the `jst` footprint function to extract pin count from ZH-specific strings (e.g. `zh7`).
* Ensured ZH variant is properly returned in output and handled in all relevant logic branches. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-de396367ba8a34cbf02f41b8ff546dc8155eca135368a5beee7c2e204ad54055L178-R239) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-de396367ba8a34cbf02f41b8ff546dc8155eca135368a5beee7c2e204ad54055R255)

**Input normalization and parsing improvements:**

* Updated input normalization in `footprinter.ts` to automatically convert strings like `zh7` to the expected `jst_zh7` format for easier user input.

**Testing:**

* Added tests for JST ZH connectors, including snapshot SVG tests for `jst7_zh` and `zh7` inputs to verify correct footprint generation.

**Code consistency and cleanup:**

* Minor import ordering and formatting improvements for consistency in `footprinter.ts` and test files. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-a537890bf0756533ee070e1320d0245f16bc5dfbe50d4e10703cbb5fa096f09aL1-R9) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-feb39d011fba13a3294314d1d7f3e0685a46e589f4d2df5e702d72746759c4f2L1-R1)

description: Adds a platform-level switch to disable all PCB DRC checks, allowing operators to globally silence netlist, placement, and routing DRC without toggling each flag individually.
body:
### Motivation

- Introduce a single platform-level switch to disable all PCB DRC checks so platform operators can globally silence netlist/placement/routing DRC without needing to toggle each fine-grained flag.

### Description

- Add reading of `platform.drcChecksDisabled` and an inherited `drcChecksDisabled` property in `Board.updatePcbDesignRuleChecks` to gate all DRC flows.
- Make `shouldRunNetlistChecks`, `shouldRunPlacementChecks`, and `shouldRunRoutingChecks` respect the new top-level `drcChecksDisabled` in addition to the existing per-check flags.
- Add a regression test `tests/root-circuit/platform-drc-checks-disabled.test.tsx` that verifies `platform.drcChecksDisabled` suppresses DRC errors alongside existing tests for the granular flags.
- Bump `@tscircuit/props` to `^0.0.490` in `package.json` and run formatting to keep the repo consistent.

### Testing

- Ran `bun update --latest @tscircuit/props` which installed `@tscircuit/props@0.0.490` successfully.

title: Move PCB trace connectivity checks to routing checks
description: Moves PCB trace connectivity validations from netlist checks to routing checks, ensuring proper classification of checks in the system.
body:
### Motivation
- PCB trace connectivity validations (`checkEachPcbPortConnectedToPcbTraces` and `checkSourceTracesHavePcbTraces`) are routing concerns and should be classified under routing checks rather than netlist checks. 

### Description
- Removed `checkEachPcbPortConnectedToPcbTraces` and `checkSourceTracesHavePcbTraces` from `runAllNetlistChecks` and added them to `runAllRoutingChecks` in `lib/run-all-checks.ts`.
- Updated the `README.md` aggregate check descriptions to reflect that those two checks are part of routing checks and that `runAllNetlistChecks` only includes `checkPinMustBeConnected` now.
- Added a test `runAllNetlistChecks excludes routing-only pcb trace connectivity checks` in `tests/lib/run-all-checks.test.ts` that asserts routing-only PCB trace connectivity checks are excluded from netlist checks and still present in routing checks.

### Testing
- Ran `bun test tests/lib/run-all-checks.test.ts` which exercised the new test but the run failed due to an existing dependency/export mismatch (`getBoardBounds` not exported by the installed `@tscircuit/circuit-json-util`) and not because of the change in this PR.
- Ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` which reported pre-existing TypeScript/type errors unrelated to the changes in this PR.
- Ran `bun run format` which completed successfully (formatting applied where applicable).

### Motivation

### Description

- Replaced detailed per-function sections with a consolidated Function Overview table in `README.md` where each entry links to the corresponding source file (for example `./lib/check-each-pcb-port-connected-to-pcb-trace.ts`).
- Added a new "Aggregate check runner functions" table documenting `runAllPlacementChecks`, `runAllNetlistChecks`, `runAllRoutingChecks`, and `runAllChecks` with links to `./lib/run-all-checks.ts`.
- Updated function names in the overview to match actual exports (for example `checkEachPcbPortConnectedToPcbTraces`) and ensured the table covers all exported checks.
- Only documentation was changed (`README.md`) and no runtime logic was modified.

### Testing

- Integrate the orientation check into placement checks so it runs with other board placement validations.

### Description

- Export the new check from `index.ts` and include it in `runAllPlacementChecks` in `lib/run-all-checks.ts` so it runs with placement checks.
- Add unit tests in `tests/lib/check-connector-accessible-orientation.test.ts` and a snapshot SVG in `tests/lib/__snapshots__/check-connector-accessible-orientation.snap.svg` to validate detection and rendering of the warning.
- Bump related dev dependency ranges in `package.json` for `@tscircuit/circuit-json-util`, `circuit-json`, and `tscircuit` and increment package `version`.

description: Hardens geometry consistency and numeric robustness across the convex-regions pipeline by ensuring outputs are derived from clamped points and replacing fixed tolerances with scale-aware values.
body: This PR hardens geometry consistency and numeric robustness across the convex-regions pipeline. - Adds `clampPointToBounds` and applies it to returned point sets. - Ensures `regions` and `hulls` are derived from the same clamped points as `pts` (both direct API and solver pipeline), eliminating mixed-coordinate outputs. - Propagates `bounds` through `TriangulateStageOutput`/`MergeCellsStageOutput` so downstream stages can apply bound-aware logic. - Replaces fixed `0.1` tolerances in `inFreeSpace` with a scale-aware epsilon based on board dimensions. - Updates SVG snapshots to reflect the stabilized, in-bounds geometry behavior.

title: Infer platedhole shape when omitted and default to pin-header circular plated hole
description: Adds functionality to infer the shape of plated-hole props when omitted, defaulting to a pin-header circular plated hole, while maintaining type contracts and improving validation.
body:
### Motivation
- Plated-hole props may be provided without a `shape` field, and consumers expect the library to infer reasonable defaults instead of failing validation. 
- Provide a sensible default used for pin headers when no distinguishing properties are present while preserving the existing `PlatedHoleProps` type contract.

### Description
- Added `inferPlatedHoleShapeAndDefaults` which examines incoming raw plated-hole props and sets `shape` based on other provided properties (e.g. `padOutline` → `hole_with_polygon_pad`, rect pad fields → `circular_hole_with_rect_pad` or `pill_hole_with_rect_pad`, diameter/pad fields → `circle`, outer width/height → `oval`/`pill`).
- Implemented a final fallback to a pin-header-like circular plated hole by defaulting to `holeDiameter = "0.04in"` and `outerDiameter = "0.1in"` when nothing disambiguates shape.
- Wired the inference into the Zod schema with `z.preprocess(inferPlatedHoleShapeAndDefaults, platedHolePropsByShape)` while keeping the discriminated union schema (`platedHolePropsByShape`) and the external `PlatedHoleProps` types unchanged.
- Replaced the previous `.refine` usage with a `.transform` on the discriminated union to normalize deprecated `innerWidth/innerHeight` into `holeWidth/holeHeight` and adjusted the inferred input type to `z.input<typeof platedHolePropsByShape>` for type checking.

### Testing
- Ran the plated-hole unit tests with `bun test tests/platedhole.test.ts` and observed all tests pass: `8 passed, 0 failed` (includes new tests for inferred circle shape, default pin-header fallback, and pill-with-rect-pad inference).
- Ran TypeScript typecheck with `bunx tsc --noEmit` and it completed successfully.
- Regenerated docs and types with `bun scripts/generate-component-types.ts`, `bun scripts/generate-manual-edits-docs.ts`, `bun scripts/generate-readme-docs.ts`, and `bun scripts/generate-props-overview.ts` and ran `bun run format` to apply formatting; these steps completed without errors.

title: Fix 404s when loading KiCad footprints from mod cache

body:
### Motivation
- The server was returning 404s for many valid KiCad footprint and 3D model paths because it only fetched a single rewritten path and did not encode special characters in path segments.
- The evaluator script that checks all KiCad footprint names uncovered these failures, so the server needs to try alternate path variants and encode URLs to reliably resolve assets.

### Description
- Update `app/[...anypath]/fetch-file.ts` to try multiple fetch candidates: first a normalized library folder variant (e.g. append `.pretty` / `.3dshapes`), then fall back to the original raw path if the normalized path returns non-200.
- URL-encode path segments before building the GitLab raw URL so names containing reserved characters (commas, etc.) are fetched correctly.
- Add tests in `tests/wrl-serving.test.ts` to verify WRL fetch behavior, the `.pretty` fallback flow, and URL-encoding behavior for special-character footprint names.
- Run project formatting which also updated minor formatting in `lib/kicad-converter.ts` and `renovate.json` (no logic changes).

### Testing
- Ran typecheck with `bunx tsc --noEmit`, which succeeded.
- Ran formatter with `bun run format` to normalize files.
- Ran unit tests with `bun test tests/wrl-serving.test.ts` and `bun test tests/kicad-converter.test.ts`, and all tests passed (3 tests in `wrl-serving.test.ts`, 6 tests in `kicad-converter.test.ts`).

title: Try alternate CAD model URLs (OBJ/WRL/STL → GLTF → STEP) before showing load error
description: Attempts to load alternate CAD model URLs sequentially before displaying an error, improving the robustness of model loading in the viewer.
body:
### Motivation
- Some CAD model URLs (for example WRL) can 404 or fail to load while other URLs for the same component (STEP/GLTF) are valid, so surface the fallback URLs before showing the error cube.

### Description
- Build an ordered candidate list of model renderers (OBJ/WRL/STL → GLTF/GLB → STEP) and attempt them sequentially instead of failing on the first loader error by adding `fallbackModelComponents` in `src/AnyCadComponent.tsx`.
- Add per-component state (`fallbackModelIndex`, `lastModelError`) and reset logic to retry from the first candidate when inputs change.
- Wrap the active candidate in `ThreeErrorBoundary` plus a small `ModelLoadErrorReporter` that advances the index on loader errors so the next candidate is tried, and only re-throws the final error once all candidates are exhausted.
- Preserve existing behavior for `model_jscad` and `footprinter_string` rendering when no external candidate URLs exist.

### Testing
- Ran `bun test tests`, which executed 12 tests: 10 passed and 2 failed; the failures are pre-existing assertion mismatches in `tests/preprocess-circuit-json.test.ts` and are unrelated to these changes.
- Ran `bunx tsc --noEmit`, which succeeded with no type errors.
- Ran `bun run format`, which formatted files successfully.

body:
### Motivation
- Ensure the snake_case `show_courtyards` flag (used by some callers) is accepted and propagated into the `circuit-to-svg` PCB renderer so courtyard visibility works regardless of casing.

### Description

### Testing

title: Add documentation pages for <courtyardcircle />, <courtyardrect />, and <courtyardoutline />
description: Add user-facing documentation for the courtyard drawing elements to assist footprint authors in declaring clearance areas for circular, rectangular, and polygon courtyards, including runnable examples.
body:
### Motivation
- Provide user-facing documentation for the three courtyard drawing elements so footprint authors can declare clearance areas for circular, rectangular and arbitrary polygon courtyards. 
- Include runnable `CircuitPreview` examples to demonstrate common usages (outlined, filled, dashed) for each element.

### Description
- Add `docs/elements/courtyardcircle.mdx` with an overview and two `CircuitPreview` examples showing basic and filled circular courtyards and usage of props like `pcbX`, `pcbY`, `radius`, `strokeWidth`, and `isFilled`.
- Add `docs/elements/courtyardrect.mdx` with an overview and two `CircuitPreview` examples showing basic and dashed rectangular courtyards and usage of props like `width`, `height`, `strokeWidth`, and `isStrokeDashed`.
- Add `docs/elements/courtyardoutline.mdx` with an overview and two `CircuitPreview` examples showing polygon outlines (including a filled example) and usage of `points`, `strokeWidth`, and `isFilled`.

### Testing
- Ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` to typecheck the project and it completed successfully.
- Ran `bun run format` to format documentation files and the formatter completed successfully.

title: Add direct pin-to-pin distance output for placement analysis
description: Adds direct pin-to-pin distance output for placement analysis to show distances between pins with direct connections.
body:
### Motivation
- The placement analysis should show pin-to-pin distances for pins that have exactly one direct pin-to-pin (not pin-to-net) connection so users can see short physical connections like jumper/shorted pins.

### Description
- Added a new `direct_pin_to_pin_distance` analysis line-item type in `lib/types.ts` to model direct pin distances.
- Implemented pad-to-port mapping and direct-connection detection in `lib/analyzeComponentPlacement.ts`, including a `getPinCenter` helper and logic that finds `source_trace` entries with exactly two `connected_source_port_ids` and no `connected_source_net_ids`, computes the Euclidean distance, and emits `direct_pin_to_pin_distance` items.
- Rendered the new line-item in `lineItemToString()` as `X -> Y distance: Zmm` in `lib/analyzeComponentPlacement.ts` so `getString()` shows the requested format.
- Added a regression test `tests/examples/example01-R1-direct-pin-distance.test.ts` asserting the output for `R1`, and made small TS-safety adjustments (`if (!trace) continue`) and formatting changes.

### Testing
- Ran `bun test tests/examples` which produced 4 passing tests (including the new `example01-R1-direct-pin-distance` test). 
- Ran `bunx tsc --noEmit` which completed successfully with no type errors. 
- Ran `bun run format` which formatted files; formatting succeeded.
